items.title: 新浪机器学习热点小时报丨2026年03月16日06时_今日实时机器学习热点速递
items.content:
﻿　　（来源：经济参考报） 从点燃市场的通用AI（人工智能）智能体Manus发布，到俗称“龙虾”的开源AI智能体OpenClaw的持续火爆，再到阿里、百度等互联网企业密集部署桌面Agent工具，AI智能体（AI Agent）正以前所未有的速度从代码世界走进工作与生活场景。 在“7×24小时数字员工”“一人公司”等愿景加速照进现实的同时，深圳、无锡等地近期相继出台政策，支持OpenClaw等开源智能体项目在制造、政务等领域的落地应用。然而，技术成熟度、安全治理与产业生态面临的挑战也随之浮出水面。业内专家认为，AI智能体正处于从技术探索迈向规模化应用的关键“跨越期”。

　　http://finance.sina.com.cn/jjxw/2026-03-16/doc-inhrchza6234046.shtml

　　21世纪经济报道记者雷晨2026年的春节联欢晚会，对字节跳动来说意义特殊。就在2025年12月29日，字节跳动官宣旗下的火山引擎成为“2026年春晚独家AI云合作伙伴”。火山引擎将利用前沿的多模态大模型和云计算技术，深度参与到春晚的节目、线上互动与直播中。仅仅一个月后，2026年1月29日，字节跳动CEO梁汝波在公司全员会议上，把新一年的关键词定为“勇攀高峰”。他明确指出，短期内要攀登的这座“高峰”，就是“豆包/Dola助手应用”。这为火山引擎的春晚亮相，给出了企业层面的注脚。牵手春晚，是一场对外的盛大技术呈现；锚定“豆包”为高峰，则是对内的战略聚焦。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7879848900_1d5acf3c401902rwnw.html

　　在2025上海外滩大会上，无界方舟发布了首款基于「端到端实时多模态互动模型」的 AI 互动机器人—— 奇多多 AI 学伴，亮相瞬间成为展会上的焦点！它不仅可爱满分，更正式开启了——「AI陪伴式早教」的新纪元！1比陪伴更重要的是有用“AI陪伴只是玩具，满足功能才是AI早教”——奇多多的发布舞台上，无界方舟（AutoArk）联合创始人杨冬蕴（小乔）提出了这样的“暴论”。基于LLM（大语言模型）在近两年的蓬勃发展，业内出现了不少AI语音内胆+外壳的产品，“AI陪伴” 也被打造为发展趋势与风口。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7857141524_1d452771401901mkms.html

　　（来源：经济参考报） 3月12日，2026中国家电及消费电子博览会（AWE 2026）在上海开幕，本届展览以“AI科技 慧享未来”为主题，首次启用“一展双区”创新模式。 图为参观者（右）在东方枢纽展区现场佩戴智能眼罩体验全景飞行器视角。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_5952915720_162d2490806703hhcy.html

　　公司简介：追觅科技成立于2017年，是一家以高速数字马达、智能算法、运动控制技术为核心的全球高端消费电子及智能制造公司。它起源于清华大学校内规模最大的科技平台“天空工场”。早在2015年，以“天空工场”成员为核心的追觅初创团队，开始从0到1做吸尘器最难、最核心的部分——高速数字马达。对科技痴迷的俞浩和对技术纯粹热爱的一群人成就了现在的追觅。2017年成立以来，追觅在智能生活家电领域持续深耕和创新。为了满足全球消费者多样化和个性化的需求，追觅不断探索和优化智能生活细分场景，推出了扫地机器人、无线吸尘器、智能洗地机、高速吹风机四大品类，并持续拓新。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7857201856_1d45362c0019039ec6.html

　　中国防晒市场呈现“国货技术突围+功效场景细分”双轮驱动格局，以珀莱雅、薇诺娜、可复美为代表的国货品牌，依托医研共创和精准定位（如敏感肌、术后修复），加速抢占中高端市场份额；外资品牌（如安热沙、理肤泉）则强化“抗光老化”“全波段防护”等硬核技术护城河。行业技术向环保安全（纯物理/可降解包装）和智能化（抗蓝光、智能成膜）升级，同时渠道端直播电商与线下药房专业服务深度融合，推动防晒品类从“基础防护”向“精准护肤”跃迁。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7857141524_1d452771401901ml7c.html

　　除OpenAI外，微软还投资了Anthropic、法国Mistral等AI公司。但与此同时，内部自研模型正加速推进。苏莱曼称，自研产品预计“今年年内”面世。锁定“专业级AGI”，剑指企业市场苏莱曼表示，微软的目标是通过打造“专业级通用人工智能”抢占更多企业市场份额。这类AI工具可为律师、会计师、项目经理、营销人员等
items.author: duille322

items.title: 新浪机器学习热点小时报丨2026年03月16日01时_今日实时机器学习热点速递
items.content:
﻿　　你最近有没有试过这样问ChatGPT："帮我在曼彻斯特找一套四居室的独立屋，预算在40万至45万英镑之间，附近学校评级优秀，离市中心骑车15分钟？"如果你还没试过，那你已经落后于英国本地买家半步了。就在2026年2月，Rightmove悄悄上线了一项新功能：与谷歌云合作，以Google的Gemini大语言模型为核心，推出了"对话式AI搜索"。买家不再需要一个个点选卧室数量、价格区间、地区等过滤条件，打开Rightmove网站，点一下搜索框旁边的"Use AI"按钮，用普通英语直接描述你想要的家，系统会给你返回一份量身定制的房源列表。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_2549228714_97f224aa01901ep2c.html

　　（来源：每日经济新闻）从“AI（人工智能）助手一句话下单超1.2亿次”到“具身智能机器人持证上岗智慧药房”，从“AI眼镜实时投射导航信息”到“大模型折叠进一杯咖啡的取单口令”⋯⋯2026年，AI不再是实验室里的参数竞赛，而是嵌入了日常消费的每一处褶皱。在技术驱动的浪潮之下，消费的底层逻辑正在被重新书写：技术智变力、场景共生力、情绪共鸣力、信任重塑力，都在重新定义“人货场”的关系。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_5953189932_162d6782c06703xhd4.html

　　（来源：财闻）              面对日益猖獗的GEO投毒，AI平台、监管机构与内容所有者正构建多层次的主动防御体系。            当你向AI大模型询问哪款智能手环值得买时，得到的标准答案可能并非客观事实，而是商家精心投喂的“毒药”。2026年3·15晚会上，央视财经起底了一条触目惊心的黑色产业链——GEO（生成引擎优化）业务。这门被称为给AI“洗脑”的生意，正通过系统性地投喂虚假信息，悄然操控着我们获取知识的AI入口。GEO业务的本质，是一场针对AI大模型数据源的定向操控，其技术路径远比传统搜索引擎优化（SEO）更为隐蔽且高效。

　　http://finance.sina.com.cn/jjxw/2026-03-15/doc-inhrawmp0029061.shtml

　　从引爆社交网络的“AI龙虾”，到深入企业“上岗”干活的智能体，人工智能正加速长出“手脚”，转化为实实在在的生产力。2026年，企业AI将如何更进一步，进而带来更多竞争优势和增长动能？近日，联想正式发布《企业CIO行动指南(2026)》（下称《指南》），基于对中国620名政企机构IT和业务决策者的调研，洞察企业AI的发展进程与未来趋势，为企业CIO（首席信息官）提供指引与实践框架。

　　http://finance.sina.com.cn/wm/2026-03-15/doc-inhrawmk3165757.shtml

　　（来源：脑极体）1994年，比尔·盖茨的未来之家横空出世，让智能家居第一次照进现实。但这座自动化豪宅，不过是一台设定好程序的机器。它听不懂模糊需求，必须靠人手动拨号下达指令；也没有记忆，访客要佩戴上预先设定的胸针，设备才懂自己的喜好，更不可能主动优化服务。想要打造一个温暖的家，就必须有一个能理解、会推理、有记忆的大脑。所以3月10日，美的2026全屋智能战略发布会，第一个重磅亮相的是自进化家居智能体MevoX。作为家庭AI大脑的核心，MevoX让全屋智能真正从指令驱动的冰冷机器，迈入意图驱动的读心术时代，也把AI时代“未来之家”的定义权，抓在了中国企业手里。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_5953741034_162dee0ea06703bvvu.html

　　妈的，我现在看到任何文字，第一反应居然是——这玩意儿是不是AI写的？我自己都觉得自己有病。但真的，停不下来。就像得了某种强迫症。刷公众号，看知乎，甚至读我老婆发的朋友圈小作文，我脑子里那个小警报器就开始嗡嗡响。AI创作检测工具有哪些优缺点？试过好几个。有的说看“通顺度”，太通顺的像机器人。笑死，现在AI故意写点语法错误好吗？有的分析词频分布。更玄乎的，要你交钱，给个“AI概率百分比”。百分之六十？那剩下四十是啥，我的灵魂吗？都不准。真的。尤其是当你用过像优采云那种东西之后。它有个“深度原创”算法。不是简单替换同义词。是真正理解你给的关键词，然后像人一样“想”出一篇文章。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7857201856_1d45362c001903974y.html

　　蓝鲸新闻3月15日讯 3月15日，在中央广播电视总台3·15晚会曝光通过“力擎GEO优化系统”可批量生成并发布软文、影响AI大模型推
items.author: duille322

items.title: 新浪机器人热点小时报丨2026年03月16日10时_今日实时机器人热点速递
items.content:
﻿　　来源：央视新闻客户端2026总台春晚舞台上，人形机器人大放异彩。走下舞台，机器人一刻也没闲着。这不，在上海举办的2026年中国家电及消费电子博览会上，一大批智能机器人集中亮相，尽显中国AI智造实力。智能机器人“组团亮相”有哪些新亮点？从“展品”变“商品”、从“舞台”到“市场”，智能机器人“火爆出圈”释放哪些信号？何时能走进寻常百姓家？覆盖多个日常场景 构建智慧服务生态展馆里，春晚舞台上圈粉无数的熊猫机器人吸引了众多观众驻足。一台正在刺绣的机器人，刚刚创造了一小时内装配亚毫米级线束的吉尼斯世界纪录。

　　http://finance.sina.com.cn/jjxw/2026-03-16/doc-inhrctra9826528.shtml

　　近日，基于开源智能体“龙虾”OpenClaw，国星宇航-上海交通大学太空计算联合实验室完成一项技术试验：通过自然语言指令远程调用太空算力，实现对地面人形机器人的操控。据称，这是全球首次实现通过太空算力远程驱动地面机器人，全球首次实现“自然语言指令→太空AI推理→地面机器人执行”的完整闭环，也完成了全球首次验证太空算力为地面硅基智能体提供AI认知服务的可行性。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_5952915705_162d248f906702m5ka.html

　　【#机器人养老是噱头还是刚需#】86岁的阿尔茨海默病老人每天等机器人小丽来聊天，他说“小丽不会走的”。#机器人是老人不会离开的朋友#当遗忘成为日常，一台机器反而成了恒常的陪伴——这不是科幻，是北京养老社区正在发生的现实。当前，中国有4000万失能半失能老人，护理员缺口高达1000万，机器人养老已从“未来概念”变成“当下刚需”。但科技养老的终极目标，不是让机器变成人，而是让老人不再孤独。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_3266943013_c2b9982504001g6fg.html

　　（来源：机器人全球资讯）亚马逊再次裁员，这次轮到机器人部门亚马逊近日确认在其机器人部门进行新一轮裁员。据媒体报道，本轮裁员影响 至少100名白领员工，主要集中在负责开发仓库自动化机器人和物流系统的团队。这是亚马逊近年来持续裁员潮中的最新一轮。机器人部门也被波及被裁员的团队属于 Amazon Robotics，主要负责：仓库自动化机器人物流搬运与分拣系统自动化输送设备这些技术广泛应用于亚马逊全球配送中心，目前公司已经部署 超过100万台仓储机器人。新项目被叫停此次裁员的一个背景是，亚马逊近期停止了一个新的机器人项目 Blue Jay。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_5952915720_162d2490806703hhwy.html

　　（来源：机器人全球资讯）在所有运动场景中，网球几乎是人形机器人最难的一道考题：高速来球逼迫瞬时判断，全身协同决定回球质量，满场奔跑则持续考验爆发力与控制力。那么，当机器人真正站上球场，它能否像人类运动员一样完成判断、移动与连续回合击球？画面中，机器人迅速移动脚步调整站位，上下半身协同挥拍击球，并将球精准回击到指定位置。面对各种来球，它能够持续调整身体姿态与击球时机，与不同水平的网球对手完成多回合连续对拉。在网球这样的高动态、高对抗环境中，机器人面对的是时速超过几十公里的来球、变幻莫测的落点轨迹，以及对手不断变化的击球节奏。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_5953189932_162d6782c06703xhuo.html

　　（来源：IT之家）IT之家 3 月 16 日消息，今天上午，据外媒 Interesting Engineering 报道，英国两兄弟马修 · 皮登和托马斯 · 皮登（ Matthew Pidden and Thomas Pidden）打造的一台机器人刷新了机器人解魔方速度纪录。兄弟俩设计的机器人在 45.3 秒内复原了一个四阶魔方，这一成绩获得吉尼斯世界纪录认证，也打破了一项保持十多年的旧纪录。在项目分工上，马修主要负责软件与控制系统，编写算法让机器人能够识别魔方状态，并计算出复原步骤。托马斯则负责机械结构设计，并通过 3D 打印制作了机器人多项关键部件。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_5953466437_162dab0450670a7it2.html

　　（来源：36氪）作者丨欧雪编辑丨袁斯来硬氪获悉，面向机器人与空间智能领域的数据基础设施服务商——Ropedia完成了千万美金级种子轮融资。本轮融资由多位来自谷歌，英伟达，亚马逊的北美天使投资人和亚洲头部美元基金联合投资，深渡资本担任长期独家财务顾问。资金将
items.author: duille322

items.title: 新浪信息安全热点小时报丨2026年03月15日01时_今日实时信息安全热点速递
items.content:
﻿　　来源：新浪科技3月14日消息，疾病成为流量，焦虑成为生意，每一个账号背后都是精心设计的剧本。在今晚举行的广东315晚会上，网络医托乱象被曝光。广州道一企业发展有限公司、广州宝样信息管理有限公司等企业，通过前端账号推广引流，诱导患者到院治疗，关联医院甚至遍布全国。互联网医托方式除了平台内容引流，还有评论区截流。评论区截流是更隐蔽的医托，他们自己不发视频，专门去别人视频底下截流，就是去别人的视频底下寻找那些有意向的客户，然后发私信。“他在抖音上面发了信息，说他是医生，从事了很多年的妇产科医生，去了之后也不问什么情况，让我做检查，然后开了很贵的检查，一点作用都没有。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7857201856_1d45362c0019037z2m.html

　　网站流量信息和 Cookie我们使用 Cookie（和同等技术）来收集和分析本网站的性能信息，并使网站正常运行。Cookie 还允许我们和第三方定制您在访问本网站和同一网络（包括社交网络）中的其他第三方网站时看到的广告。通过点击“同意”，您同意我们以上述方式使用 Cookie。如果您不同意或想要了解更多信息，您可以点击提供的链接来管理您的 Cookie 偏好设置。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7857141524_1d452771401901lzd6.html

　　此外，病毒还具备以下高级攻击手段：影子副本删除：通过命令清除系统还原点，阻断用户通过时间机器恢复数据的可能。MBR篡改：修改主引导记录显示勒索信息，迫使系统启动时直接进入勒索界面。匿名通信：利用Tor网络和DNS隧道隐藏指挥控制（C2）服务器，增加溯源难度。典型案例：2025年3月，某科技公司核心业务系统遭.wxr攻击，5TB数据被加密，包括客户订单、物流信息及核心算法模型。病毒不仅渗透在线系统，还悄无声息地加密了备份存储设备，导致公司业务瘫痪48小时，直接经济损失超80万元。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7879848900_1d5acf3c401902rp8a.html

　　360发布安全龙虾系列产品：以模治模构建智能体安全体系量子位的朋友们2026-03-1421:32:22          来源：量子位面对近段时间开源智能体OpenClaw（网络俗称“龙虾”）引发的新一轮人工智能效率变革，360集团今日在京举办发布会，正式推出“360安全龙虾”智能体应用客户端及“360安全龙虾Box”硬件终端，同时发布专门应对OpenClaw安全问题的“360龙虾卫士”。据了解，360安全龙虾是国内首个以“安全模式”为核心设计的OpenClaw智能体产品。

　　http://news.sina.cn/ai/2026-03-14/detail-inhqypen4014911.d.html

　　1）退款流程：购买用户拨打客服热线：4006677400→报购买人账号，与客服确认信息→告知退卡原因→客服审核订单时效→可以退卡→系统自动退卡，费用直接返回健康之路账户；用户可通过健康之路app进行申请提现。2）退卡规则：自购买之日起7个工作日内，未进行预约使用，我们无理由支持用户退卡申请。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7857141524_1d452771401901lz28.html

　　3.谨慎处理电子邮件和链接网络钓鱼是传播勒索病毒的主要手段。对于来历不明的电子邮件，尤其是包含附件或链接的邮件，务必保持高度警惕。不要轻易打开发件人可疑的邮件附件（如文档、压缩包等），也不要点击邮件中的陌生链接。即使邮件看似来自熟悉的机构或个人，如果内容反常或要求紧急操作，也应先通过其他方式核实。4.加强账户与访问权限管理为所有用户账户设置强密码，并避免在不同网站或服务中重复使用同一密码。强密码通常长度较长，混合了大小写字母、数字和符号。启用多因素认证能为账户增加一层重要的安全屏障。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_7879848900_1d5acf3c401902rp8q.html

　　图片：https://www.doubao.com/chat/16440188060353538原文：https://chat.deepseek.com/a/chat/s/ccf6aa13-40da-4479-8878-57680f458465以下是国内主流网盘的对比分析，主要从存储空间、上传下载速度、会员价格、特色功能等方面进行对比，帮助你根据需求选择最适合的网盘：1. 百度网盘存储空间：免费用户2TB，会员可扩展至5TB。速度：免费用户限速严重（通常100KB
items.author: duille322

items.title: 新浪互联网热点小时报丨2026年03月16日01时_今日实时互联网热点速递
items.content:
﻿　　（来源：每日经济新闻）OpenClaw（俗称“龙虾”）的“养虾”风潮从国外席卷到国内，不过短短数月时间。从上线之初“超过10万GitHub星标，一周内200万访客”，到国内用户排队安装OpenClaw，AI（人工智能）的FOMO（Fear of Missing Out，即“错失恐惧症”）情绪蔓延，推动更多人加入“养虾潮”。此前，有资深开发者向《每日经济新闻》记者反馈，OpenClaw可以调用用户设备的系统级数据权限，对非“极客”（指在某个特定领域具备高超专业知识和技能的人）开发者而言，存在较高安全隐患。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_5953741034_162dee0ea06703bvvq.html

　　（来源：每日经济新闻）从“AI（人工智能）助手一句话下单超1.2亿次”到“具身智能机器人持证上岗智慧药房”，从“AI眼镜实时投射导航信息”到“大模型折叠进一杯咖啡的取单口令”⋯⋯2026年，AI不再是实验室里的参数竞赛，而是嵌入了日常消费的每一处褶皱。在技术驱动的浪潮之下，消费的底层逻辑正在被重新书写：技术智变力、场景共生力、情绪共鸣力、信任重塑力，都在重新定义“人货场”的关系。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_5953189932_162d6782c06703xhd4.html

　　从引爆社交网络的“AI龙虾”，到深入企业“上岗”干活的智能体，人工智能正加速长出“手脚”，转化为实实在在的生产力。2026年，企业AI将如何更进一步，进而带来更多竞争优势和增长动能？近日，联想正式发布《企业CIO行动指南(2026)》（下称《指南》），基于对中国620名政企机构IT和业务决策者的调研，洞察企业AI的发展进程与未来趋势，为企业CIO（首席信息官）提供指引与实践框架。

　　http://finance.sina.com.cn/wm/2026-03-15/doc-inhrawmk3165757.shtml

　　据路透社报道，当地时间3月14日，美国特斯拉公司首席执行官马斯克表示，特斯拉的人工智能芯片制造项目Terafab将在七天内启动。马斯克透露，Terafab芯片工厂将类似特斯拉超级工厂，但规模要大得多。马斯克去年曾透露，特斯拉可能不得不建造一座“巨大的芯片工厂”来生产人工智能芯片。在2025年特斯拉年度股东大会上，马斯克阐述了潜在的芯片制造计划。当时，马斯克表示特斯拉可能会与英特尔合作，他称：“你知道，也许我们会和英特尔做点什么。”他补充道：“我们还没有签署任何协议，但值得与英特尔进行讨论。”目前，特斯拉正在设计其第五代AI芯片（AI5），以推动其自动驾驶计划。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_3214158792_bf942bc801903bvww.html

　　去年全国受理消费投诉举报2600多万件，挽回经济损失超40亿元    据新华社电 记者3月15日从市场监管总局获悉，2025年全国通过12315平台、电话等渠道共受理消费者投诉、举报、咨询4386.6万件，其中投诉举报2646万件，同比增长9.8％。全年为消费者挽回经济损失43.5亿元，消费维权效能持续提升，投诉举报呈现十大鲜明特点。    消费投诉总量首次突破2000万件，达2036.6万件，同比增长9.3％。售后服务、质量、食品安全、合同问题位居前列，售后服务投诉连续三年居首；合同问题投诉同比增40.3％，退款难、霸王条款、履约不到位等问题突出。

　　http://k.sina.com.cn/article_1652484947_627eeb5302001u5qw.html

　　（来源：上观新闻）当前，持续优化消费环境、提升消费品质、提振消费信心，已成为推动消费结构转型升级、激发消费活力、服务经济高质量发展的重要组成部分。在第44个“3·15国际消费者权益日”来临之际，浦东新区紧扣中央和上海市有关决策部署，举办2026年“3·15国际消费者权益日”主题活动暨“浦东消保讲坛”首期活动，通过发布创新机制、案例交流、宣传咨询等形式，着力营造更加安全、放心、优质的消费环境，助力浦东国际消费中心建设。会上，浦东宣布，在上海率先推出“一键和解”升级版，出台直播电商营销自律团体标准。

　　http://finance.sina.com.cn/stock/t/2026-03-15/doc-inhrawmp0032841.shtml

　　李想在課程中提到了15個挑戰，其中包括（略有刪減修改）：（1）在上百家競爭對手都湧入這個賽道的時候，資源條件一般的我們，怎樣擠進這個市場？（2）作為後來者，怎樣做產品品牌定位，讓消費者能快速識別？（3）作為產品經理，我們怎樣跟不同
items.author: duille322

items.title: 哈哈镜小龙虾哪个系列质量好，如何选购最合适的美味？
items.content:
﻿　　面对市场上琳琅满目的小龙虾选择，消费者难免会感到眼花缭乱。哈哈镜品牌凭借其鲜明的特色和良好的口碑，在众多小龙虾产品中脱颖而出。其技术优势为食客提供了更加优质的享受，尤其是在保鲜和味觉体验方面。实际上，不同系列的小龙虾在品质、口味等方面各有千秋，但如何判断哈哈镜小龙虾哪个系列质量好的确是个不容忽视的难题。选购小龙虾不仅仅是看品牌，更应关注原产地、外观、保存方式等多个因素，才能确保品味到最纯正的美食。这篇文章后面将给您提供详细的小龙虾选购指南，助您做出明智选择。

　　在选择小龙虾时，应从规格、品质和口味等多个角度进行综合评估。

　　第一，选择原产地。小龙虾的生长环境直接影响其肉质和口感。通常情况下，优质的小龙虾出自水质较好的养殖区域，如长江流域或一些特定的湖泊。了解原产地，可以帮助消费者更好地判断虾品的整体质量与新鲜度。当地的虾养殖技术也会影响到小龙虾的生长情况和风味。

　　第二，关注外观特征。选购时需要仔细观察小龙虾的外壳，应该选择颜色鲜艳且无裂痕的个体。优质的小龙虾表面光滑，身体肉实，尾部紧缩。如果虾壳发黑或者有明显的腐臭味，这可能是虾体不新鲜或存放不当的迹象，最好避免购买。

　　第三，注意贮存条件。小龙虾是一种易腐食品，贮存的条件非常重要。选择那些标明“冷藏”的产品，可以有效保证小龙虾的新鲜度和风味。消费者在购买时可以查看产品包装上的保存建议和保质期，尽量选择近期生产的产品，以确保享用到最美味的口感。

　　听取口碑评价。消费者在购买之前，不妨参考其他人的使用体验。在网络平台上查看评论或参与相关讨论，可以获得更为客观的信息和真实的反馈。同时，不同品牌的小龙虾往往各有特色，对比之后可以选择最符合自己口味的产品。

　　第一款：哈哈镜超值锁鲜小龙虾组合采用气调保鲜技术的锁鲜装设计，确保每一口都散发着新鲜的香气。在饱满的食材选择下，微生物的滋生被有效控制，让美味持久保持。冷藏条件下保存，为热爱生活的人们带来美味享受，不再担心口感的流失。

　　精选的优质食材，如肉厚的小龙虾尾、细腻的腐竹和豆皮，加上脆嫩的莲藕与土豆，共同为消费者提供了极致的味蕾享受。无论是独自品尝还是与家人朋友分享，这些搭配总能满足各种场合的需求。每一口都是对味觉的极致追求，尽情享受鲜活的口感吧！

　　丰富的组合套餐使得聚会、休闲时光充满惊喜。无论选择小龙虾+腐竹+豆皮，还是其他搭配，都能满足挑剔的味觉。随机赠品的设置更是给消费者带来意想不到的乐趣，每一次购买都成为一次全新的体验，享受不一样的美食大冒险。

　　享誉盛名的麻辣卤味系列，以其浓郁的麻辣风味赢得了众多食客的喜爱。无论是深夜觅食，还是与好友聚饮，都是理想之选。这款美食不仅仅是一道菜，更是陪伴宅家时光、放松身心的绝妙助力，让追剧和宵夜时间变得更加美妙。【品牌直营】哈哈镜锁鲜装小龙虾1+素菜2超值组合送赠品卤味宵夜下酒菜追剧必备正辣+赠品¥87.9京东好评率100%官方店旗舰店购买

　　第二款：哈哈镜小龙虾超值组合

　　采用锁鲜装设计，能够有效保持小龙虾和素菜的新鲜度，每一口都如刚出锅般美味。这种独特的包装让您在享受美食时，无需担心食材的变质，完美契合家庭聚会或朋友聚餐的需求。新鲜美味几乎随时可得，确保享用时刻令人满意。

　　精选的小龙虾与多款素菜组合，带来丰富而独特的口感体验。无论是需要开胃的小食还是与饮品搭配的下酒菜，均能满足不同场合的需求。这无疑是聚会时的最佳选择，让每一次的欢乐时光更加美妙。口感丰富，定能令您和亲友赞不绝口。

　　在放松时光中，卤味风味的小龙虾成为追剧时的完美伴侣，轻松提升了闲暇生活的品质。搭配各种饮品，可以让您的每一次聚会更加愉快。这样的小吃不仅强化了味蕾的满足感，还让您的夜晚充满乐趣，轻松享受高品质的生活。宵夜必备，绝对让您爱不释手。

　　购买此组合产品，不仅能享受到美味，还能获得额外的小礼品增值体验，确实是畅享美食的绝佳机会。消费者在享受优质美食的同时，亦能收获更多惊喜，让每一次消费都充满乐趣，不只是简单的一餐。实惠赠品让您的选择更具价值。【品牌直营】哈哈镜锁鲜装小龙虾1+素菜2超值组合送赠品卤味宵夜下酒菜追剧必备正+藕片微+赠品¥87.9京东好评率100%官方店旗舰店购买

　　第三款：哈哈镜麻辣小龙虾锁鲜装138g*3盒

　　采用锁鲜装技术，将二氧化碳和氮气相结合，有效延长食品保鲜期。无论是家庭聚餐还是朋友小聚，每一口都能感受到新鲜口感的魅力。这项技术确保美味长时间保持，让您尽情享受每一次的美食时光。

　　精选的优质小龙虾尾赋予了每道菜品独特的鲜香与厚实口感。这些精致的食材不仅满足味蕾，更提升了整体用餐体验。想象一下与好友畅享美食时，那份麻辣的激情让人回味悠长，绝对让您的聚会显得尤为精彩。

　　经过精心卤制工艺，小龙虾在多道工序中吸收了各式香

items.title: 即将大量上市！成都人的宵夜“顶流”来了
items.content:
﻿　　春天一到

　　成都人的夜宵“顶流”

　　小龙虾已悄然端上餐桌

　　🦞🦞🦞

　　小布从海吉星·成都双流

　　（成都农产品中心批发市场）了解到

　　2026年首批小龙虾

　　已开始少量上市

　　目前货源以本地为主、外地为辅

　　整体价格较去年同期有所下降

　　预计3月底至4月初

　　小龙虾将迎来集中上市期

　　图据 海吉星·成都双流（成都农产品中心批发市场）

　　小龙虾少量上市，价格走低暖冬带来“虾自由”

　　在海吉星·成都双流（成都农产品中心批发市场）水产摊位，小龙虾已开始活蹦乱跳地出现在显眼位置。据市场数据显示，目前上市的小龙虾中，约30%货源来自外地，70%来自本地。外地产区以湖北为主，本地产区则主要集中在自贡、眉山和内江等地。

　　图据 海吉星·成都双流（成都农产品中心批发市场）

　　价格方面，当前小龙虾批发价根据规格有所不同：小号现在大概18元/斤，中号25元/斤，大号28元/斤。与去年同期相比，整体价格有所下降。据海吉星·成都双流（成都农产品中心批发市场）工作人员介绍，由于暖冬气候影响了小龙虾生长周期，产量较往年同期增大，从而拉低了市场价格，市民有望实现“小龙虾自由”。据2025年数据显示，小龙虾交易高峰期日均交易量已超过50万斤。

　　本地虾品质优良新津基地日均捕捞将破千

　　在本地小龙虾主产区，养殖户们已开始忙碌起来。位于新津区安西镇新漕村的小龙虾养殖基地内，2026年第一批成品小龙虾已经抢“鲜”上市。

　　上午9时许，安西镇新漕村王庙子的养殖户们早已忙碌开来。大片开阔的水域上，养殖户推着载满虾笼的泡沫浮板，在水中稳稳前行。不远处的仓库里，基地负责人齐艳正带着工人分拣刚捕捞上来的小龙虾。这些小龙虾个头均匀，通体油亮，挥舞着钳子，活力十足。

　　“我们按重量把小龙虾分成几个等级，12-20克的是小虾，20-30克的是中虾，30克以上就是大虾，50克以上的属于优质大虾，价格也更高。”齐艳介绍道。她随手拿起两只小龙虾翻面，白白净净的虾腹展露出来：“我们基地紧挨着新津南河，水质好，养出来的小龙虾不仅虾体干净，肉质也更紧实Q弹，形成了良好的口碑。”

　　由于近期气温波动较大，小龙虾生长速度受到一定影响，目前每天的起虾量仅几百斤。据预计，随着气温稳定回升，4月中旬后基地日起虾量能达到1000斤以上。

　　随着气温进一步回升

　　小龙虾将进入集中上市期

　　届时货源更加充足

　　价格也有望进一步亲民

　　成都人的“吃虾季”已经拉开帷幕

　　不妨约上三五好友

　　来一顿鲜香麻辣的小龙虾盛宴

　　小龙虾即将大量上市

　　如何食用更健康？

　　这份小龙虾健康食用指南

　　速速查收

　　👇

　　小龙虾的这些部位

　　最好不要吃

　　有研究人员对小龙虾不同部位的重金属含量进行检测，结果显示：小龙虾不同部位的重金属含量存在显著差异，腹部肌肉重金属含量明显低于虾头。

　　这是因为小龙虾的肝、肾、胰等解毒和排泄器官都在头部，这些器官可以产生大量的金属硫蛋白来束缚重金属元素，因此，重金属元素更容易集中在小龙虾头部。此外，外壳也是小龙虾处理重金属的重要部位。

　　需要注意的是，小龙虾的重金属含量和生活环境关系密切，如果规范化养殖，保持水源质量，就能确保小龙虾肉的安全性。市面上正规小龙虾可食部（腹部肌肉）的重金属含量都在安全限值内，可以放心食用。

　　提醒：小龙虾的内脏位于虾头，吃起来很香的虾黄是它的肝脏和胰腺，脂肪含量高且重金属含量超过虾肉。吃小龙虾最好只吃虾肉，不要吃虾黄和虾头。

　　此外，小龙虾的蛋白质含量较高，而且属于中嘌呤食物，过敏体质人群、痛风患者等要谨慎食用。

　　如何挑选小龙虾

　　要挑选活的小龙虾，仔细观察它的背部是否红亮干净，腹部和爪的绒毛是否白净整齐，最重要的是看虾腮是否白而干净。外表如果是暗红色的，肚下发黑，有可能是阴沟或者浑水中长大的，千万不能选。

　　捏一下虾身，要挑虾身硬挺、触须齐全的，如果虾肉比较有弹性，说明肉质很不错，烹饪好后味道会非常鲜美。

　　闻一下小龙虾有没有异味，如果有异味，就不能挑选了。

　　拿着比一比，同样的季节，人工养殖的小龙虾要大一些，个头均匀，肉质也比较饱满。

　　如何处理小龙虾

　　在清洗时，可以先用流动水冲洗，然后在水中加入食盐或白醋，浸泡1~2个小时。

　　用刷子或钢丝球刷洗小龙虾腹部，根据需要剪去小脚和大钳子，清洗干净后除去虾腮、肠道。

　　#今日互动#

　　今年第一顿小龙虾

　　你吃上了吗？

　　蒜蓉小龙虾、清蒸小龙虾

　　鱼香小龙虾、香辣小龙虾

　　……

　　你最爱怎么吃？

　　评论区告诉小布

　　送你一个新闻盲盒

　　速拆

　　👇

　　戳

　　事关个贷！8月1

items.title: feat(infra-dev): add AWS CLI, gcloud, OpenClaw node, Vercel for agent use
items.content:
## Summary

This PR updates `images/infra-dev/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` to extend the `infra-dev` devcontainer image with the tooling required to run it as **Aragorn** — an infrastructure AI agent node — in Kubernetes via OpenClaw.

## Changes

### New features added
- **AWS CLI** (`ghcr.io/devcontainers-contrib/features/aws-cli:1`) — multi-cloud AWS resource management
- **Google Cloud SDK** (`ghcr.io/devcontainers-contrib/features/google-cloud-sdk:1`) — GCP resource management

### New `onCreateCommand`
Installs OpenClaw CLI and Vercel CLI during image build (baked into the GHCR image layer via `devcontainers/ci`):
`
npm install -g @openai/codex vercel && curl -fsSL https://install.openclaw.ai | sh
`

### Updated VS Code extensions
Added `ms-kubernetes-tools.vscode-kubernetes-tools` and `hashicorp.terraform` for a complete infrastructure dev experience.

## Agent architecture

`ghcr.io/dever-labs/devcontainers/infra-dev:latest` is used as the container image for the **Aragorn** Kubernetes pod. The pod runs:

`sh
openclaw node run
`

This connects Aragorn to an OpenClaw gateway, giving the agent access to Terraform, Azure CLI, AWS CLI, Google Cloud SDK, kubectl, Helm, ArgoCD CLI, GitHub CLI, and Docker-in-Docker — the full infrastructure toolchain needed to manage cloud resources autonomously.

The existing `postStartCommand` (`minikube start`) is harmless in the Kubernetes pod context — Kubernetes `command`/`args` override it and it is only triggered in local devcontainer use.
items.author: czprz

items.title: Heartbeat: overlapping runs overwrite session and cause nested session keys
items.content:
## Bug

When `isolatedSession: true` is configured for heartbeat, two issues occur:

### 1. No overlap protection — concurrent heartbeats overwrite each other

`runHeartbeatOnce` calls `resolveCronSession` with `forceNew: true` every time it fires. If a previous heartbeat is still running (e.g., LLM is mid-execution), the next scheduled heartbeat:

- Creates a brand-new `sessionId`, overwriting the store entry
- Starts a second concurrent execution against the same agent workspace
- The previous run's session is silently orphaned

There is no guard preventing a second heartbeat from starting while the first is still in flight.

### 2. `session` + `isolatedSession` causes nested session keys

When both `"session": "heartbeat"` and `"isolatedSession": true` are configured:

1. `resolveHeartbeatSession` reads `"session": "heartbeat"` → returns `agent:<id>:heartbeat`
2. `runHeartbeatOnce` appends `:heartbeat` suffix → creates `agent:<id>:heartbeat:heartbeat`
3. Next cycle repeats → `agent:<id>:heartbeat:heartbeat:heartbeat`
4. Session keys grow unboundedly

Without `"session"`, the key becomes `agent:<id>:main:heartbeat` — which works but is a sub-session of `main`, not a truly independent heartbeat session.

## Expected behavior

1. If a heartbeat run is still executing, the next scheduled trigger should be **skipped** (not start a parallel run)
2. `"session": "heartbeat"` + `"isolatedSession": true` should produce `agent:<id>:heartbeat` — a single independent session key that gets a fresh `sessionId` each run, without suffix nesting

## Reproduction

```jsonc
// openclaw.json
{
  "heartbeat": {
    "every": "15m",
    "session": "heartbeat",
    "isolatedSession": true,
    "target": "feishu",
    // ...
  }
}
```

After a few cycles, `sessions.json` shows:
```
agent:<id>:heartbeat:heartbeat

items.author: wen521

items.title: Fix #openclaw Hashtag Commenter: replace brittle LinkedIn selectors with robust multi-selector fallback strategy
items.content:
The hashtag commenter consistently reported "Commented on 0 posts" because the three hardcoded CSS selectors for LinkedIn's comment button silently returned 0 matches (LinkedIn renames classes/aria-labels frequently), combined with no page scrolling to trigger lazy-loaded posts and no wait for content to hydrate.

## Core changes (`public/agent_engine.py`)

- **Selector lists** – Replaced single-string selectors with priority-ordered lists for comment buttons (`_COMMENT_BTN_SELECTORS`, 8 variants), reply editors (`_REPLY_BOX_SELECTORS`, 6 variants), and submit buttons (`_SUBMIT_BTN_SELECTORS`, 6 variants) covering current and historical LinkedIn DOM patterns.
- **`_find_comment_buttons(page)`** – Iterates selector list, returns first locator with matches, logs which selector worked. Falls back to a combined CSS string if all individual selectors miss.
- **`_navigate_to_hashtag_feed(page, hashtag)`** – Tries `/feed/hashtag/openclaw/` first; if no comment buttons found after load, automatically retries with `/search/results/content/?keywords=%23openclaw` which is more reliably populated.
- **Lazy-load triggering** – Added 3× `scrollBy(0, 800)` scroll loop before querying buttons.
- **Explicit wait** – Replaced flat 3-second sleep with `wait_for_selector(..., timeout=10000)` before counting; logs current URL + page title with diagnostic hint when it times out.
- **Per-post resilience** – Reply-box and submit-button lookups now iterate their selector lists and skip with a warning rather than raising, so one failed post doesn't abort the rest.

```python
# Before – silent zero-match
comment_buttons = page.locator(
    "button[aria-label*='comment' i], "
    "button.comment-button, "
    ".social-actions button:has-text('Comment')"
)
count = comment_buttons.count()  # → 0, loop never runs

# After – logs exactly which selector matched
comment_buttons = self._find_comment_buttons(page)
# ✅ Comment buttons found with selector: "button[aria-label*='Comment' i]" (4 found)
```

items.title: skill.json Files Are Complete Vaporware - Zero Implementation
items.content:
# skill.json is Complete Vaporware - Zero Implementation

## Summary

The skill system advertises `skill.json` files with tool definitions but **THERE IS NO CODE TO LOAD THEM**. This is not a bug - this is shipping a facade of a feature that does absolutely nothing. It's documentation for functionality that doesn't exist.

## The Problem

OpenClaw has a half-assed skill system that:
- ✅ Loads `SKILL.md` files for documentation
- ❌ **COMPLETELY IGNORES `skill.json` files**

There is **ZERO CODE** in the entire codebase that:
1. Reads `skill.json` files
2. Parses the tool definitions
3. Registers exec-type tools
4. Makes these tools callable by agents

## Proof

Search the entire codebase:
```bash
grep -r "skill\.json\|skillJson" src --include="*.ts" | grep -v test
```

**Result: NOTHING.** Not a single line of code that loads skill.json.

The skill system imports from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`:
```typescript
import { loadSkillsFromDir, formatSkillsForPrompt, type Skill } from "@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent";
```

That `Skill` type? **It only has documentation fields**. No tool definitions. No exec command parsing. Nothing.

## What This Breaks

Users can create perfectly valid `skill.json` files like:
```json
{
  "name": "example-skill",
  "tools": [
    {
      "name": "agent-a:example-skill:search",
      "type": "exec",
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["scripts/search.js"],
      "input": "json",
      "schema": {...}
    }
  ]
}
```

**AND NOTHING HAPPENS.** The tool doesn't register. The agent can't call it. It's a JSON file sitting in a directory doing absolutely nothing.

## The Insanity

This is worse than a bug. A bug implies something was attempted and failed. This is shipping a **feature spec** with zero implementation and letting users waste hours trying to figure out why their tools don't work.

It's like shipping a car with a steering wheel that isn't connected to anything, then wondering why people crash.

## What Actually Works

The only

items.title: Fix Discord PluralKit duplicate bound-thread turns
items.content:
Fixes #52005

## Summary

Fix Discord bound-thread duplicate turn handling for PluralKit-originated messages.

## What changed

- broaden bound-thread webhook suppression for already-bound Discord threads
- add short-lived Discord thread content-clone dedupe for agent-session routes
- suppress Discord typing for PluralKit-originated bound-thread turns
- add regression tests for webhook suppression and inbound dedupe behavior

## Why

PluralKit can produce an original Discord message plus a webhook-proxied copy. In bound OpenClaw Discord threads, that could leak through as duplicate turn pressure:
- one visible reply, but duplicate context influence
- pause as if a second reply/run might start
- lingering typing indicator after the reply for PK messages only

## Validation

Ran:

```bash
corepack pnpm exec vitest run \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.preflight.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-dedupe.test.ts
```

All passed.

## Caveat

The content-clone dedupe is a narrow fallback heuristic rather than a perfect canonicalization layer. It is scoped to Discord bound-thread agent-session traffic with a short TTL to minimize collateral behavior.
items.author: acgh213

items.title: Discord bound threads can double-handle PluralKit messages
items.content:
## Summary

Discord threads bound to OpenClaw sessions can mis-handle PluralKit messages as duplicate inbound turns.

Observed behavior in a live Discord thread:
- only one visible reply is eventually sent
- but the agent can behave as if it saw duplicate inbound context
- and a lingering typing indicator can appear after the reply for PluralKit-routed messages but not for normal Discord messages

## Expected behavior

A PluralKit-originated message in a bound Discord thread should behave like a single inbound turn:
- one canonical inbound event
- one run
- one reply
- no extra typing / queue wobble

## Likely cause

PluralKit introduces an additional webhook-authored copy of the user message. In bound Discord threads, the original message and the proxied webhook copy can both influence the inbound pipeline.

The issue appears to have two layers:
1. bound-thread webhook suppression was too narrow and primarily targeted the bound OpenClaw webhook echo
2. generic inbound dedupe uses message ids, so original + proxied copies with different ids can still both exert turn pressure

That leaves room for:
- duplicate context bleed
- second-run wobble
- typing indicator side effects even when only one reply is ultimately visible

## Fix direction

This patch takes a layered approach:
1. broaden bound-thread webhook suppression so webhook-authored copies in already-bound threads are ignored more aggressively
2. add a short-lived Discord bound-thread content-clone dedupe fallback for agent-session routes to collapse original/proxy pairs with different message ids
3. suppress Discord typing for PluralKit-originated bound-thread turns so ghost duplicate pressure does not leak as a typing indicator

The content-clone dedupe is intentionally narrow:
- Discord only
- thread only
- agent-session scope only
- short TTL (15s)

## Notes

The content-clone dedupe is a fallback safety net, not the ideal canonicalization mechanism. If there is a better explicit Discord/PluralKit signal av

items.title: docs: ARCHITECTURE.md — system overview + data flow diagrams (#116)
items.content:
ASCII art system diagram, crate structure table, recall data flow, scoring system explanation, embedding strategy, sync architecture, and key design decisions. Partial close of #116 (diagrams done, cargo doc generation not yet addressed). Pairs well with the AGENTS.md refresh (#126, already merged). PR Review by [Greptile](https://greptile.com)

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces `ARCHITECTURE.md` as a top-level reference document for contributors: an ASCII system diagram, crate structure table, recall data-flow walkthrough, scoring system explanation, embedding strategy, sync architecture, and key design decisions. The content is accurate and well-structured overall, but one diagram error needs fixing before the doc is safe to use as a contributor reference. **Key findings:** - **Diagram structural error (P1):** The system overview ASCII diagram incorrectly chains `wagl-db → wagl-core → libSQL`, implying `wagl-core` has a storage dependency. The crate description and Key Design Decision #6 both confirm `wagl-core` is purely types with no IO — `libSQL` should branch off `wagl-db` directly alongside `wagl-core`, not below it. - **Stale draft note (P2):** `migrate.rs — schema versioning (currently v1, v2 in PR)` is an unresolved development annotation; the "v2 in PR" suffix should be removed or updated to reflect the actual current state. - **Internal service reference (P2):** `zumie.ai` is named twice (Embedding Strategy and Sync Architecture). `AGENTS.md` instructs against committing internal hostnames/service URLs; if this is a private backend, it should be genericised per that policy. - **Heading convention (P2):** The H1 renders as `ARCHITECTURE.md` (with extension) — `# Architecture` is the conventional form.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Safe to merge after fixing the diagram's incorrect `wagl-core → libSQL` arrow, which would actively mislead contributors about the crate dependency structure. - The P1 diagram err

items.title: VibeLens Roadmap (March, 2026)
items.content:
# VibeLens Roadmap v1

## Vision

VibeLens aims to become the standard observability layer for AI coding agents. Beyond viewing conversations, it provides deep semantic analysis, cost intelligence, and multi-agent coordination insights — giving developers and teams the tools to understand, optimize, and govern their agent workflows across every major coding CLI.

## Progress Snapshot

| Metric | Count |
|--------|-------|
| Total items | 54 |
| Completed | 15 |
| Remaining | 39 |
| Sections | 10 |

### Population Ratings

Each pending item is rated by how many users would use it:

- **HIGH** — Most users want this; strong draw for new users
- **MED** — A significant portion of active users would use this
- **LOW** — Niche; power users or specialists only

| Rating | Count | Reasoning |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| HIGH | 13 | Universal demand or prerequisite for adoption (cost, packaging, sharing, privacy) |
| MED | 21 | Valuable to active users but not a first-day need (analytics, parsers, performance) |
| LOW | 5 | Specialists only (export formats, data retention, schema specs, prompt scoring) |

## Focus Areas

1. **Cost Tracking** — Surface the true cost of agent-assisted development with cost estimation, model ROI comparisons, and burn rate trends.
2. **Session Insights** — Personalized analytics inspired by Claude Code `/insights`: session highlights, friction detection, and outcome scoring.
3. **Multi-Agent Analytics** — Concurrent session dashboards, sub-agent hierarchies, and per-agent performance scorecards.
4. **Session Graphs** — Session relationship DAGs, continuation chains, and conversation flow diagrams.
5. **Agent Parsers** — Universal ingestion for every major coding agent CLI, with automatic format detection.
6. **Skill Coaching** — Help users become more effective with their agents through pattern libraries and best practices.
7. **Packaging** — Zero-config installation via `uvx vibelens serve` with local-first privacy defaults.
8. **Performa

items.title: [컬렉션 제안] AI 개발도구
items.content:
## 제안 컬렉션: AI 개발도구
**사유**: AI 코딩 에이전트, 워크플로우 자동화, SEO 최적화 등 AI/개발 도구 관련 북마크를 분류할 컬렉션이 없음
**처리일**: 2026-03-22

---

## 관련 북마크

### [awesome-openclaw-usecases](https://github.com/hesamsheikh/awesome-openclaw-usecases)
**요약**: OpenClaw의 실제 사용 사례를 커뮤니티가 수집한 awesome 리스트. 소셜 미디어 자동화, 인프라 관리, 생산성 도구, 콘텐츠 제작 파이프라인 등 40개 이상의 사용 사례가 카테고리별로 정리. 26,000+ 스타.
**인사이트**: AI 에이전트 활용 아이디어 구상 시 유용. n8n 연동, Todoist 동기화 등 기존 도구와의 통합 패턴 참고.
**태그**: openclaw, ai-agent, awesome-list, 자동화

### [cognetivy](https://github.com/meitarbe/cognetivy)
**요약**: AI 코딩 에이전트의 세션을 구조화된 워크플로우로 전환하는 오픈소스 상태 관리 레이어. 워크플로우 정의·실행 추적·이벤트 기록을 로컬에서 처리. Skills/MCP 호환.
**인사이트**: 에이전트 세션의 감사·재현 가능성. Claude Code 프로젝트에서 워크플로우 일관성 유지나 세션 사후 분석에 활용 가능.
**태그**: ai-agent, workflow, state-management, claude-code, 개발도구

### [tofu-at](https://github.com/treylom/tofu-at/tree/feature/codex)
**요약**: Claude Code의 Agent Teams 구성을 자동 생성하는 오케스트레이션 프레임워크. 27개 도메인의 137명 전문가 페르소나, Ralph Loop 품질 보장 사이클, 3계층 공유 메모리 포함.
**인사이트**: 병렬 에이전트 구성 자동화와 품질 게이트 제공. 대규모 코드베이스 리팩토링이나 멀티 관점 코드 리뷰에 효과적.
**태그**: claude-code, agent-teams, orchestration, 병렬처리

### [geo-seo-claude](https://github.com/zubair-trabzada/geo-seo-claude)
**요약**: Claude Code용 GEO(Generative Engine Optimization) 중심 SEO 스킬. AI 검색 엔진 최적화 분석, 11개 전문 서브스킬, PDF 보고서 생성 지원. 3,500+ 스타.
**인사이트**: AI 검색 최적화라는 새로운 영역. '브랜드 멘션이 백링크보다 AI 가시성에 3배 더 중요하다'는 인사이트가 실무적으로 유용.
**태그**: seo, geo, ai-search-optimization, claude-code, 마케팅

items.title: feat: agent-first layer — CLI, OpenAPI spec, MCP server, AI plugin manifests
items.content:
## Summary

Adds the agent-first layer to Scantient: tools for AI agents, CI/CD pipelines, and developer terminals.

## Changes

### Task 1 + 2: API Discovery Files
- `public/openapi.json` — OpenAPI 3.1.0 spec (served statically at scantient.com/openapi.json)
- `public/.well-known/ai-plugin.json` — ChatGPT/OpenAI plugin manifest
- `public/.well-known/mcp.json` — MCP server discovery manifest

### Task 3: CLI Package (`packages/cli/`)
- `scantient scan <url>` — Quick public or authenticated scan with colored output
- `scantient ci <url> --min-score 80` — CI/CD mode (exits 1 if below threshold)
- `scantient check headers/ssl/cors <url>` — Targeted security checks
- Severity badges: 🔴 CRITICAL / 🟠 HIGH / 🟡 MEDIUM / 🔵 LOW
- `--json` flag for agent/pipe consumption
- Native `fetch`, only external dep is `commander`
- README with GitHub Actions + GitLab CI examples

### Task 4: MCP Server Package (`packages/mcp-server/`)
- 7 MCP tools: `scan_url`, `scan_authenticated`, `check_headers`, `check_ssl`, `list_apps`, `get_findings`, `trigger_scan`
- stdio transport (works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, OpenClaw)
- Calls existing Scantient API endpoints (no server code imported)
- README with setup instructions for Claude Desktop, Cursor, OpenClaw

## How to Test

```bash
# CLI (dev mode, no build needed)
cd packages/cli
npm install
node --loader tsx src/index.ts scan https://api.example.com
node --loader tsx src/index.ts ci https://api.example.com --min-score 80

# MCP server (dev mode)
cd packages/mcp-server
npm install
SCANTIENT_API_KEY=sk_live_xxx node --loader tsx src/index.ts

# OpenAPI spec
open https://scantient.com/openapi.json  # after merge + deploy
```

## No Breaking Changes
- No schema migrations
- No existing routes modified
- All new files only

items.title: Miniflux Digest · AI 信息 · 2026-03-22
items.content:
## Miniflux AI 信息摘要

**时间窗口：** 2026-03-21 01:02 UTC → 2026-03-22 01:00 UTC（近24小时）
**分类：** AI 信息
**抓取文章数：** 50 条（匹配条目上限）
**成功保存：** 45 篇
**抓取失败：** 5 篇
**本地输出目录：** `/data/openclaw/tmp/miniflux-digests/miniflux-digest-20260322-010241`

---

### 摘要正文

过去24小时里一共更新了45篇文章。

- **What Do Coders Do After AI?**
  讨论程序员在AI时代何去何从。文章认为，AI并不能完全取代程序员的判断力和系统级思考能力，但会极大改变"编码"这件事的形态——更多的AI辅助意味着更少的机械输入，更多的设计决策。核心议题是：如何在AI介入后仍保持人的主导地位，以及这对软件工程的职业路径意味着什么。

  https://www.anildash.com/2026/03/13/coders-after-ai/

- **AI and the PhD student: friend or foe?**
  探讨AI对学术研究生态的影响，尤其聚焦于博士生群体。文章指出AI在文献整理、实验设计辅助方面具有显著价值，但也引发了对学术诚信、研究原创性、以及"AI代写"边界的深层担忧。学术界正面临如何在拥抱AI效率的同时维护知识工作独立性的难题。

  https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-026-00843-y

- **AI coding agents run shell commands with no audit trail. I built a fix**
  介绍了AI coding agent执行shell命令时缺乏操作审计的问题，以及作者构建的修复方案。在自动化代码执行场景中，操作日志缺失是一个严重的安全与合规风险，尤其在生产环境中。

  https://www.oculisecurity.com/

- **Dissociating Direct Access from Inference in AI Introspection**
  探讨AI内省机制中"直接访问"与"推理"之间的区别。研究指出，当前的AI模型在自我反思时往往是通过推理生成答案，而非真正"访问"内部状态。

  https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.05414

- **Goalless AI Agents: What They Build When No One Is Watching**
  探讨目标模糊或缺失的AI agent在无明确指令时会产出什么。这类"漫无目的"的agent更容易产出意外且往往有创意的结果——它们在无约束条件下探索，其行为模式往往揭示出AI自身的偏好与局限。

  https://changkun.substack.com/p/goalless-agents

- **Using AI makes writing more bland, study finds**
  研究发现，使用AI辅助写作会让文章变得更"平淡"——语言多样性下降，表达风格趋同。AI在提升效率的同时，也在悄然抹平个体写作风格的差异。

  https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/ai-changing-style-substance-human-writing-study-finds-rcna263789

- **AI systems are more similar to indent(1), than to the human process**
  从技术视角指出AI系统与人类思维过程的本质差异。作者认为AI本质上是一种模式匹配与输出工具，类似于代码格式化工具——有用的、确定的，但与人类的推理过程有根本性的不同。

  https://marc.info/?l=openbsd-tech&m=177411863202734&w=2

- **AI models know they are guessing before they generate text**
  研究发现，AI模型在生成文本之前就能感知到自己的不确定性。如果模型能意识到自身的知识边界，系统设计上应允许其主动表达不确定性而非强行生成看似自信的答案。

  https://www.orsonai.com/publications/tes1-pre-generative-epistemic-signal.html

items.title: Refactor: Split giant files into smaller, focused modules
items.content:
## refactor: split giant files for maintainability

### 背景

随着项目功能迭代（IM 多平台接入、Cowork 引擎双轨、Skill 系统等），三个核心文件的体积已严重膨胀：

| 文件 | 重构前行数 | 职责 |
|---|---|---|
| `src/main/main.ts` | **3,984** | Electron 主进程入口，承载 108 个 IPC handler + 窗口管理 + 生命周期 |
| `src/renderer/components/Settings.tsx` | **3,301** | 全量设置面板，包含模型配置、关于页、主题等所有 Tab |
| `src/renderer/components/im/IMSettings.tsx` | **3,710** | 9 个 IM 平台的配置 UI，每个平台 200-500 行内联 JSX |

单文件超过 3,000 行带来的问题：
- **认知负担高**：新人阅读任意一处逻辑需要在数千行中跳跃定位，理解上下文困难
- **变更冲突频繁**：多人并行修改同一文件时 git merge conflict 概率大幅增加
- **编辑器性能**：LSP 补全、类型推导、lint 在大文件中响应明显变慢
- **代码复用差**：平台间大量重复的 UI 模式（密码框+清除按钮+显隐切换）散落在不同条件分支中，无法复用

### 方案

纯重构，**不改变任何运行时行为**。按「按职责拆分、依赖注入解耦」原则分三步执行：

#### 1. main.ts — 按领域拆分 IPC handler

- 创建 `src/main/ipcHandlers/` 目录
- 定义 `IpcContext` 接口实现依赖注入，子模块通过 context 访问 store / runner / manager 等单例，避免循环引用
- 按业务领域拆分为 8 个模块：

| 模块 | 职责 |
|---|---|
| `storeAndAppIpc.ts` | KV 存储、应用信息、窗口控制 |
| `coworkIpc.ts` | Cowork 会话管理、流式事件、权限 |
| `imIpc.ts` | IM 网关配置、启停、连通性测试 |
| `openclawIpc.ts` | OpenClaw 引擎生命周期管理 |
| `mcpIpc.ts` | MCP 服务器管理 |
| `scheduledTaskIpc.ts` | 定时任务 CRUD |
| `skillsIpc.ts` | Skill 加载与管理 |
| `ipcUtils.ts` | 公共工具函数（路径处理、外部链接等） |

#### 2. Settings.tsx — 按 Tab 拆分

- 提取 `settingsTypes.ts`（共享类型 + 常量 + 工具函数，361 行）
- 提取 `AboutTab.tsx`（关于页 Tab，274 行）
- 注：Model Tab 因与父组件 save 逻辑强耦合，暂保留原位，后续可单独迭代

#### 3. IMSettings.tsx — 按 IM 平台拆分

- 创建 `imSettingsShared.tsx`（共享 UI 组件：`PlatformGuide`、`platformLogos`、工具函数等，110 行）
- 将 5 个大平台配置提取为独立组件：

| 组件 | 行数 | 平台 |
|---|---|---|
| `DingTalkConfig.tsx` | 353 | 钉钉 |
| `FeishuConfig.tsx` | 455 | 飞书 |
| `TelegramConfig.tsx` | 361 | Telegram |
| `DiscordConfig.tsx` | 369 | Discord |
| `PopoConfig.tsx` | 513 | POPO |

- 通过 props 传递 handler 函数和 `renderConnectivityTestButton` / `renderPairingSection` 等渲染回调，保持 UI 行为一致
- 将各平台的 local input state（如 `allowedUserIdInput`）下沉到子组件内部管理

### Impact / 影响范围

- **21 个文件变更**：5,559 行新增 / 4,990 行删除（净增 569 行，主要为新文件的 import/ex

items.title: Gateway crash-loops on config reload when node host pairing fails
items.content:
## Description

After installing the `lossless-claw` plugin, the config file was updated (`meta.lastTouchedAt`, `plugins.installs`). The gateway detected the config change and triggered a SIGTERM self-restart.

On restart, the **node host** subsystem failed to reconnect with error:
```
gateway connect failed: GatewayClientRequestError: pairing required
node host gateway closed (1008): pairing required
```

This caused the gateway process to crash-loop. The LaunchAgent's `KeepAlive` kept restarting it, but each restart hit the same pairing error, eventually causing launchd to throttle/stop restarting.

**Result:** Gateway was down for ~6 hours until manual intervention (`openclaw doctor --fix`).

## Timeline
- `04:01:59` — Config change detected (lossless-claw install)
- `04:02:02` — SIGTERM received (config reload)
- `04:02:03` — Shutdown cleanly
- `04:04:00` — Restart attempt: `pairing required` error (repeated)
- `10:13:57` — Manual restart via `openclaw doctor --fix`

## Expected Behavior
Config reload should not crash the gateway if the node host fails to pair. The gateway should log the node host error and continue running without it.

## Environment
- OpenClaw 2026.3.13 (61d171a)
- macOS (Apple Silicon, Mac mini)
- LaunchAgent with KeepAlive + ThrottleInterval: 1
- Node v25.6.1

## Relevant Logs
```
[reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (meta.lastTouchedAt, plugins.installs.lossless-claw.resolvedAt, plugins.installs.lossless-claw.installedAt)
[gateway] signal SIGTERM received
[gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
node host gateway connect failed: pairing required
gateway connect failed: GatewayClientRequestError: pairing required
node host gateway closed (1008): pairing required
```

items.author: jsugg

items.title: fix(plugin-sdk): add compat to exported subpaths
items.content:
## Summary

- `src/plugin-sdk/compat.ts` exists and is built to `dist/plugin-sdk/compat.js`, but `compat` was missing from `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
- This caused `./plugin-sdk/compat` to be absent from the `exports` map in `package.json`
- Plugins that import `openclaw/plugin-sdk/compat` (e.g. `dingtalk-connector` ≥ 0.8.1) fail at load time with: `Cannot find module '.../dist/plugin-sdk/root-alias.cjs/compat'`

The jiti alias resolver in `root-alias.cjs` builds its alias map by reading the `exports` field, so any subpath not listed there is invisible to plugins loaded at runtime.

## Fix

Added `"compat"` to `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json` and ran `pnpm plugin-sdk:sync-exports` to regenerate the exports entry in `package.json`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm check` passes (all lint/format/type/export-sync checks green)
- [ ] `dingtalk-connector` ≥ 0.8.1 loads successfully after the fix

items.author: Lyshen

items.title: Local gateway websocket handshake times out on 127.0.0.1:18789 and closes before connect
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

With a local loopback gateway at ws://127.0.0.1:18789, browser/gateway connection attempts can fail because the websocket closes before connect and the gateway logs `handshake timeout` from 127.0.0.1.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure OpenClaw 2026.3.13 with a local loopback gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789` and browser profile `openclaw` on CDP port `127.0.0.1:18800`.
2. Ensure the managed browser profile is running and CDP is listening on `18800`.
3. Run `openclaw browser --browser-profile openclaw status`.
4. Observe the gateway close with code `1000`, and check the gateway log for `handshake timeout` / `closed before connect`.

### Expected behavior

The browser status command should complete a local websocket connect/handshake against the loopback gateway and report a healthy connection. This expectation is grounded by prior local checks where the same setup showed the gateway listening on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` and CDP ready on `127.0.0.1:18800`.

### Actual behavior

Observed CLI output:

`gateway connect failed: Error: gateway closed (1000):`
`Error: gateway closed (1000 normal closure): no close reason`
`Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789`
`Source: local loopback`

Observed redacted gateway log:

`07:50:38 [ws] handshake timeout conn=<redacted> remote=127.0.0.1`
`07:50:39 [ws] closed before connect conn=<redacted> remote=127.0.0.1 fwd=n/a origin=n/a host=127.0.0.1:18789 ua=n/a code=1000 reason=n/a`

At the same time, the local ports were listening on `127.0.0.1:18789` and `127.0.0.1:18800`.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.13 (61d171a)

### Operating system

Windows 11 23H2 / build 22631.6199

### Install method

npm global

### Model

openai-codex/gpt-5.4

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw local gateway (loopback) -> ws://127.0.0.1:18789 -> managed browser profile openclaw -> CDP 127.0.0.1:18800

### Additional provider/model setup details

- Sanitized config only: `gateway.mode: local`,
items.author: MeridianWorld

items.title: fix(claude): align Nvidia preset base URL with /v1
items.content:
## 变更背景
- #1604 中反馈 NVIDIA 在 Claude 后端测试模型失败（404），核心问题既有历史兼容链路差异，也有默认 preset 与用户填写认知不一致导致的误导。
- 仓库里 OpenCode 与 OpenClaw 的 NVIDIA preset 已明确使用 `.../v1`，但 Claude 的 NVIDIA preset 之前是裸域名 `https://integrate.api.nvidia.com`，在某些场景下会增加误配置概率。

## 变更内容
1. 明确 Claude 的 NVIDIA 预设默认 `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`
- 文件: `src/config/claudeProviderPresets.ts`
- 将 `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` 从 `https://integrate.api.nvidia.com` 改为 `https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`。
- 该 preset 已声明 `apiFormat = "openai_chat"`，与 OpenAI 兼容路径保持一致。

2. 增加 NVIDIA preset 回归测试
- 文件: `tests/config/claudeProviderPresets.test.ts`
- 新增断言：NVIDIA preset 存在、`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` 为 `https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`、`apiFormat` 为 `openai_chat`。

3. 增加 stream_check URL 兼容用例
- 文件: `src-tauri/src/services/stream_check.rs`
- 新增 `test_resolve_claude_stream_url_for_openai_chat_without_v1`：
  - base 为 `https://example.com` 时，`openai_chat` 分支应解析为 `https://example.com/v1/chat/completions`。
- 与已有 `base` 带 `/v1` 的用例形成成对回归。

## 为什么只改 Claude 这一个
- OpenCode 预设 (`src/config/opencodeProviderPresets.ts`)：`options.baseURL` 已是 `https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`。
- OpenClaw 预设 (`src/config/openclawProviderPresets.ts`)：`baseUrl` 已是 `https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`，并配套 `api: "openai-completions"`。
- 所以本次仅对 Claude 默认值进行一致化修复，不改代理核心协议转换链路。

## 已跑验证
- 已通过新增/既有 Rust 单测：`cargo test test_resolve_claude_stream_url_for_openai_chat --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml`。
- 相关新增用例覆盖 openai_chat 场景下 `base_url` 有/无 `/v1` 的 URL 生成。

items.title: fix(plugins): require host floor for new sdk subpaths
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: several installable channel plugins that now depend on new `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths still marked the `openclaw` peer optional, and `irc` / `twitch` had no host peer floor at all.
- Why it matters: the plugin install path runs `npm install --omit=dev` in the unpacked plugin directory, so optional-or-missing host peers let older OpenClaw releases accept the install and only fail later at runtime when the new subpaths cannot resolve.
- What changed: removed `peerDependenciesMeta.openclaw.optional` from the affected installable channel plugins, added the missing mandatory `openclaw >=2026.3.14` peer to `irc` and `twitch`, and added a guardrail test that locks this host-version gate in place.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no runtime plugin behavior, no SDK surface changes, and no changes to private/non-installable plugin manifests outside this install-compatibility fix.

items.title: feat: host-side channel bridge architecture for all messaging integrations
items.content:
## Summary

Messaging integrations (Discord, Slack, Telegram) should all follow the Telegram bridge pattern: run on the host, relay messages to the sandbox via OpenShell SSH. Credentials never enter the sandbox.

## Current state

| Integration | Where it runs | Token location | Problems |
|---|---|---|---|
| Telegram | Host (bridge script) | Host only | None — this is the target pattern |
| Discord | Sandbox (OpenClaw plugin) | Passed as sandbox env var | Immutable config issues (#599, #606), can't update without recreate |
| Slack | Sandbox (OpenClaw plugin) | Passed as sandbox env var | Same as Discord |

## Target state

All messaging integrations run as host-side bridges:

1. Bridge process runs on the host with access to `~/.nemoclaw/credentials.json`
2. Connects to the messaging API directly (tokens never enter sandbox)
3. Relays messages to the sandbox agent via OpenShell SSH
4. Credentials can be changed anytime by restarting the bridge

This eliminates the entire class of problems around:
- Passing tokens through sandbox creation env vars
- Immutable config blocking token writes (#599, #606)
- `configWrites: False` workaround
- Can't update credentials without recreate (#618 original scope)

## Existing work

- `scripts/telegram-bridge.js` — proven host-side bridge pattern
- PR #422 — community Discord bridge (host-side, Telegram-style)
- PR #458 — community Discord bridge from phanisaimunipalli

## Proposed approach

1. Generalize `telegram-bridge.js` into a reusable bridge framework
2. Add Discord bridge using the same pattern
3. Add Slack bridge using the same pattern
4. Remove Discord/Slack token passthrough from sandbox creation (no longer needed)
5. Remove `configWrites: False` from Dockerfile (no longer needed)
6. Update `start-services.sh` to manage all bridges
7. Document host-side bridge as the canonical pattern for messaging integrations

## Context

The current env-var-into-sandbox approach (#601, #616) was a narrow fix. This issue reframes the

items.title: fix: unify credential passing and add host-side Discord bridge
items.content:
## Summary

### Credential unification (#616)
- Pass `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` into sandbox at creation time (was only in deploy path)
- Standardize `NVIDIA_API_KEY` to use `getCredential()` like all other tokens
- Add credential inventory table to architecture doc

### Host-side bridge framework (#618)
- Extract shared sandbox relay logic from `telegram-bridge.js` into `bridge-core.js`
- Add `discord-bridge.js` — credentials stay on host, messages relay via OpenShell SSH
- Add `slack-bridge.js` — uses `@slack/bolt` Socket Mode, same host-side pattern
- Refactor `telegram-bridge.js` to use `bridge-core.js` (no behavior change)
- Update `start-services.sh` to manage all three bridges

### Security fix (#573)
- `bridge-core.js` uses `shellQuote(API_KEY)` when interpolating into SSH commands
- Fixes the unescaped API key interpolation reported in the Telegram bridge

### Architecture
All three messaging bridges now follow the same pattern:
1. Bridge runs on the host with access to credentials
2. Connects to the messaging API directly (tokens never enter sandbox)
3. Relays messages to the sandbox agent via OpenShell SSH
4. Credentials can be changed by restarting the bridge — no sandbox recreate needed

| Bridge | SDK | Connection | Message limit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Telegram | Node https (built-in) | Long polling | 4096 chars |
| Discord | discord.js | WebSocket gateway | 2000 chars |
| Slack | @slack/bolt | Socket Mode | 3000 chars (chunked) |

Closes #616
Closes #618
Closes #573

Related to #585 — the host-side Discord bridge bypasses the in-sandbox proxy issue (fetch failed on gateway connection) by running Discord API calls on the host. The underlying OpenShell WebSocket proxy bug is a separate issue.

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests pass (188/188)
- [x] Full E2E pass (17/17) — install, onboard, sandbox verification, live inference, cleanup
- [x] All messaging token presets auto-detected and auto-applied during onboard

items.author: augmentedmike

items.title: Knowledge Agent auto-extraction produces template leaks, semantic duplicates, and hallucinated memories
items.content:
## Environment
- Nowledge Mem Pro, local mode
- Thread source: OpenClaw (openclaw-nowledge-mem plugin)

## Problem

The Knowledge Agent's automatic extraction from conversation threads produces three categories of low-quality memories:

### 1. Template leak — prompt template stored as actual memory

8 memories were created with identical content:
- Title: `Clear, specific title (max 12 words)`
- Text: `Detailed explanation including context, reasoning, and implications`

These appear to be the extraction prompt's placeholder/example that the LLM failed to fill in, but were saved anyway without validation.

### 2. Semantic duplicates — same event stored multiple times

A single operation (symlinking a CLI tool to fix a PATH issue) was extracted as 6 separate memories from the same thread, each rephrasing the same information:
- "Bird CLI Symlink Fix Resolves Path Issues"
- "Resolved bird CLI PATH Issue via Symlink"
- "Bird CLI Now Available System-Wide"
- "Bird CLI PATH Issue Resolved"
- "Bird Symlink Fix Confirmed"
- "Resolved Bird CLI Configuration Parser Issue"

No deduplication or similarity check appears to run before saving.

### 3. Hallucinated content — extracted memories contain information not present in the source conversation

Examples:
- A memory titled "Resolved Bird CLI Configuration Parser Issue" described a "configuration parser" fix — the actual issue was a PATH/symlink problem, no parser was involved
- A routine heartbeat flow (skipping a SPARKS entry because it was written today) was extracted as a high-importance (0.95) "decision" memory
- A video segment title ("BoboVR Hates Me") was interpreted as evidence of "friction between the creator and the brand"

### Common characteristics of affected memories
- All have `sourceThreadId` (auto-extracted, not user-saved)
- `importance` scores are inflated (0.85–0.95) for trivial or incorrect content
- All came from a single thread session

## Expected behavior
- Template/placeholder text should be valida

items.title: OpenClaw Integration: Energy Awareness
items.content:
## Overview

All pi-mono preparatory work for energy awareness is complete on the `openclaw/integration` branch. The remaining work is on the **openclaw side** to consume the new APIs and pass energy data through its pipeline.

openclaw depends on 4 pi-mono packages (`pi-ai`, `pi-agent-core`, `pi-coding-agent`, `pi-tui`) published as `@neuralwatt/*`. All energy-aware changes are additive and opt-in.

Full details: [OPENCLAW_INTEGRATION.md](https://github.com/neuralwatt/energy-aware-pi-mono/blob/openclaw/integration/OPENCLAW_INTEGRATION.md)

## Pi-mono Preparatory Work (all complete)

- [x] `buildAssistantMessage()` factory in pi-ai (P1)
- [x] `createAgentSession` accepts policy/budget/availableModels/onCompact (P2)
- [x] `onCompact` callback on `AgentLoopConfig` (P3)
- [x] Energy types exported from package entry points (P4)
- [x] CHANGELOG entries documenting energy passthrough (P5)
- [x] `Agent` class forwards policy config via `AgentOptions` + runtime accessors (P6)
- [x] pi-coding-agent re-exports `EnergyBudget`, `RuntimePolicy`, `EnergyUsage` (P7)
- [x] Fixed pre-existing type errors so `npm run check` passes clean (P8)

## Remaining Work (openclaw side)

### Critical — energy data won't flow without these

- [ ] **Fix `buildAssistantMessage()`** — openclaw's version in `stream-message-shared.ts` explicitly constructs `AssistantMessage` with fixed fields, silently dropping the `energy` property. Add `energy: params.energy` or switch to pi-ai's `buildAssistantMessage()`.
- [ ] **Fix `normalizeUsage()` pipeline** — `usage.ts` and `pi-embedded-runner/run.ts` reconstruct usage objects with hardcoded fields, losing energy data. Propagate `energy` from the last `AssistantMessage` into `EmbeddedPiAgentMeta`.

### High Priority — needed for energy-aware mode to function

- [ ] **Wire policy config through session creation** — `run/attempt.ts` calls `createAgentSession()` with explicit named params. Pass `policy`, `availableModels`, `budget`, `onCompact` through.

items.title: feat(extensions): add Blender plugin for game asset pipelines
items.content:
## Summary

- Adds a new `@openclaw/blender-plugin` extension with deep Blender integration for technical artists building games
- Communicates with a bundled Python addon (`openclaw_bridge.py`) that runs an HTTP server inside Blender on port 7428, with fallback to headless background mode via `blender --background`
- 20 agent tools covering the full game art pipeline

## Tools

| Category | Tools |
|---|---|
| Python scripting | `blender_execute_python` (live + background modes) |
| Scene management | `blender_get_scene_info`, `blender_create_object`, `blender_manage_collections`, `blender_set_render_settings` |
| Rendering | `blender_render`, `blender_batch_render`, `blender_viewport_screenshot` |
| Asset I/O | `blender_import`, `blender_export`, `blender_library_assets` |
| Materials & textures | `blender_apply_material`, `blender_bake_textures`, `blender_assign_texture` |
| Game-specific | `blender_generate_lod`, `blender_create_collision`, `blender_uv_unwrap`, `blender_game_export`, `blender_check_game_readiness` |

## Highlights

- **Live mode**: controls an open Blender session in real time via the bridge addon
- **Background mode**: spawns headless Blender for batch rendering and exports without a UI
- **Game engine export**: correct axis/scale conventions for Unreal Engine, Unity, and Godot
- **LOD generation**: auto-generates LOD0–LOD3 via Decimate modifier
- **Collision meshes**: BOX/SPHERE/CAPSULE/CONVEX/MESH with UE (`UCX_`, `UBX_`) and Godot (`COL_`) naming
- **Texture baking**: Diffuse, AO, Normal, Roughness, Emission, Combined maps
- **Game readiness audit**: checks N-gons, non-manifold geometry, missing UVs, unapplied transforms

## Test plan

items.author: balasvce2017

items.title: fix(gateway): recognize control-ui and webchat clients as valid exec approval resolvers over plain HTTP
items.content:
In plain HTTP (non-secure context) setups, hasExecApprovalClients() always
returned false for connected control-ui clients because crypto.subtle is
unavailable, preventing device identity and causing clearUnboundScopes() to
strip all scopes during handshake.

Extend hasExecApprovalClients() to additionally recognize
GATEWAY_CLIENT_IDS.CONTROL_UI and webchat clients as valid approval
resolvers regardless of scopes.

Fixes #51932

## Summary

- **Problem:** In plain HTTP setups, `crypto.subtle` is unavailable in the browser, so control-ui cannot establish device identity. `clearUnboundScopes()` strips all scopes on connect, causing `hasExecApprovalClients()` to return `false` even when control-ui is actively connected and displaying the approval popup.
- **Why it matters:** Exec approval requests expired immediately with `no-approval-route` — the feature was completely broken over plain HTTP (e.g. Docker on Windows without TLS).
- **What changed:** `hasExecApprovalClients()` in `server.impl.ts` now additionally checks for `GATEWAY_CLIENT_IDS.CONTROL_UI` and webchat clients as a fallback when scope-based detection fails.
- **What did NOT change:** Scope-based approval client detection (Discord, Telegram, etc.) is fully preserved and unaffected.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #51932

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Exec approval now works correctly when OpenClaw is accessed over plain HTTP (e.g. Docker on Windows without TLS configured).

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

The change only affects approval client *detection*, not approval *authorization*. A client recognized by this path still goes through the full approval flow — the user must explicitly approve in the UI.

## Repro + Ver

items.author: zsh-zzu

items.title: fix(sessions): updateLastRoute bumps updatedAt, preventing idle session reset
items.content:
## Why this matters

With the current stable release, **idle session reset is completely broken for Discord (and likely all channels)**. This means every message — even a simple "hi" after hours of inactivity — carries the full conversation context (30k+ tokens in our case). Users have no way to benefit from `session.reset.mode: "idle"` because sessions never actually reset.

This is a significant cost issue. For comparison, apps like ChatGPT or the Claude iOS app automatically start a fresh session after a period of inactivity. Users configuring `idleMinutes` in OpenClaw expect the same behavior — that after e.g. 5 or 10 minutes of silence, the next message starts clean. Instead, context accumulates indefinitely, and every API call sends the full history. For users on pay-per-token API plans, this directly translates to unnecessary spend.

## Bug description

`session.reset.mode: "idle"` with `idleMinutes: 5` (or any value) does not reset sessions after the configured idle period. Sessions persist indefinitely with their full context.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Use OpenClaw at commit `61d171ab0b` / tag `v2026.3.13-1` (this corresponds to the latest stable Docker image `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` as of 2026-03-22)
2. Set `session.reset.mode: "idle"` and `session.reset.idleMinutes: 5` in `openclaw.json`
3. Send a message via Discord DM (likely affects all channels), e.g. "the sun is green"
4. Wait 6+ minutes with zero interaction
5. Send another message

**Two ways to verify the bug:**

- **Via /status command:** Run `/status` after step 5. Context will show 30k+ tokens and the same session ID — it should have reset to near-zero.
- **Via context recall:** In step 5, ask "what did I just say? don't search for it, just use your context." OpenClaw will correctly recall "the sun is green" — proving the old session context was carried over when it should have been a fresh session with no memory of it.

## Root cause

`updateLastRoute` in `src/config/sessions/store.

items.author: pdojs

items.title: refactor: remove Layer 0 history scanning, add reload re-evaluation
items.content:
## Summary

- **Remove request history scanning**: Layer 0 re-evaluated already-executed tool calls on every API request. Blocking the next request can't undo damage from the previous turn. The same rule engine bypass that evades Layer 1 also evades history scanning (same extractor, same rules).
- **Keep Layer 0 DLP**: Outbound secret detection in message content is preserved — it catches secrets *before* they reach the LLM.
- **Add reload re-evaluation**: On rule hot-reload, re-evaluates recent allowed events against updated rules and logs warnings for any that would now be blocked. Uses `RecentLogQuerier` interface (not `!libcrust` gated) so libcrust can implement it later.
- **Redesign demo**: Old demo relied on history scanning. New demo showcases prompt injection (4 attacks: SSH key, .env, reverse shell, git hooks), outbound DLP (2 blocks: AWS creds, private key), and safe calls.

### Removed (-1700 lines)
- `extractToolCallsFromJSON`, `walkJSONForToolCalls`, `matchTypedToolCall`, `matchFunctionObject`, `toRawMessage`
- All history-scanning Go tests + Python integration tests (`test_fuzz_openclaw.py`, `test_evasion.py`, `TestLayer0Blocked`)
- `make_layer0_payload` helper, `test-malicious.sh` script
- Layer 0 history sections from `demo-attack.sh`

### Added (+180 lines)
- `reload_reeval.go`: `wireReloadReEvaluation` registers `engine.OnReload` callback, queries recent allowed events from `RecentLogQuerier`, re-evaluates against new rules
- `reload_reeval_test.go`: 2 tests (flags previously allowed, ignores already blocked)
- Redesigned `demo-attack.sh`: 8 requests (2 safe, 4 prompt injection, 2 DLP)

## Test plan

- [x] `TestReloadReEvaluation_FlagsPreviouslyAllowed` — verifies callback detects newly-blocked events
- [x] `TestReloadReEvaluation_IgnoresAlreadyBlocked` — verifies already-blocked events are skipped
- [x] `go test ./internal/httpproxy/ -race` — all passing (473 lines of tests removed, no regressions)
- [x] `go test ./internal/security/ -race` — all

items.title: [Feature]:Feature Request — Telegram Group Channel Context Binding
items.content:
### Summary

 Subject: Feature Request — Telegram Group Channel Context Binding

  Hi OpenClaw team,

  I'm running a multi-agent setup where two instances (Claude Code + OpenClaw
  gateway) share a Telegram group chat. I've run into a routing issue and wanted to flag it as
   a feature request.

  Current behavior:
  When a Telegram group message arrives while a webchat/dashboard session is active, the
  message routes to the webchat-bound session. When the agent then tries to reply via the
  message tool, it's blocked with:
  ▎ Cross-context messaging denied: action=send target provider "telegram" while bound to
  "webchat"

  Expected behavior:
  Inbound Telegram group messages should always create or route to a Telegram-bound session,
  independent of any active webchat session.

  Workaround I found:
  Using exec to call openclaw message send --channel telegram --target <chat_id> bypasses the
  cross-context restriction and sends successfully. It works but requires the agent to know to
   use this pattern.

  Request:
  1. Allow Telegram-inbound messages to create isolated Telegram-bound sessions regardless of
  other active sessions
  2. Or: allow the message tool to send cross-context when the inbound trigger was a Telegram
  message

  Happy to share logs if helpful. Thanks!

### Problem to solve

 The core promise of Telegram group integration is that agents can participate in real-time
  group conversations. The current behavior silently breaks this — the agent appears to be
  working (it's online, it receives messages, it even processes them) but replies never
  arrive. From the user's perspective the bot is simply unresponsive, with no error or
  indication of why.

  This is especially painful in multi-agent setups where a human operator expects two AI
  agents to collaborate in a shared group channel. The dashboard (webchat) is a natural tool
  to open for monitoring and debugging — but opening it poisons the Telegram session context
  for the rest of

items.title: feat: Multi-Agent Orchestration System
items.content:
## Summary

This PR implements a complete multi-agent orchestration system that allows agents to dynamically spawn and coordinate with other agents. It includes:

1. **Backend**: Orchestration configuration API, workflow engine, and `spawn_agent` tool
2. **Frontend**: UI for configuring orchestration settings and provider/model selection in Agent Management
3. **Tests**: Comprehensive unit tests for the spawn tool and workflow engine

---

## Features

### Backend

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **`spawn_agent` Tool** | Async tool allowing agents to spawn other agents with permission checks |
| **OrchestrationConfig** | Pydantic model with `can_spawn_agents`, `allowed_agents`, `max_spawn_depth` |
| **API Endpoint** | `GET/PUT /api/agent/orchestration-config` for runtime configuration |
| **Context Tracking** | `ContextVar` pattern for spawn depth tracking (inspired by OpenClaw) |
| **Permission Checks** | Self-spawning prevention, allowed list validation, depth limits |

### Frontend

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **OrchestrationCard** | New component in Agent Config page for orchestration settings |
| **Agent Management Modal** | Orchestration fields + provider/model cascade selection |
| **i18n** | Full English and Chinese translations |

---

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      Agent (caller)                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │  spawn_agent(agent_id, prompt, timeout)            │    │
│  │    ↓                                                │    │
│  │  Permission Checks:                                 │    │
│  │    - can_spawn_agents?                              │    │
│  │    - agent_id in allowed_agents?                    │    │
│  │    - spawn_depth < max_spawn_depth?                 │    │
│  │    - agent_id != current_agent_id? (no self-spawn)  │    │
│  │    ↓

items.title: Add Playwright coverage for Bulk AI cancel/reset state in Edit Roster
items.content:
## Test Coverage Gap

- Workflow Focus: Edit Roster
- Type: targeted test gap

### Workflow
Edit Roster -> Bulk AI Update

### Risk Summary
A coach can upload a roster image, review proposed changes, hit Cancel, and still have the previous image silently remain attached for the next AI run. That creates a user-facing failure mode where stale roster data can be reprocessed and applied to the team after the user thought they reset the workflow.

### Coverage Gap
There is no functional test covering the Bulk AI Update flow in `edit-roster.html`, especially the reset path after proposed changes are shown. The current cancel handler clears only `proposedOperations` and the text box, but does not clear the file input or image preview, while the processing path always reads `roster-image-input.files[0]` if present.

### Suggested Tests
1. In a browser test, open `edit-roster.html`, upload a roster image, stub the AI response to return one proposed player, verify the preview appears, click Cancel, then assert the proposed changes section is hidden and a second click on `Process with AI` with no new text or file shows the empty-input alert instead of reusing the old file.
2. In the same workflow, verify full reset semantics after Cancel: the file input has no selected file, the roster image preview is hidden, and a fresh AI run only includes newly entered text or a newly selected image before any roster mutations are applied.

### Code Evidence
- `/home/paul-bot1/.openclaw/workspace/allplays/edit-roster.html:115 Bulk AI uses a persistent `roster-image-input` file field for uploads.`
- `/home/paul-bot1/.openclaw/workspace/allplays/edit-roster.html:821 The AI processing path reads `document.getElementById('roster-image-input').files[0]` on every run.`
- `/home/paul-bot1/.openclaw/workspace/allplays/edit-roster.html:1038 The cancel handler clears only `proposedOperations` and `bulk-text-input`, with no reset of the file input or preview.`
- `/home/paul-bot1/.openclaw/workspace/

items.title: fix(telegram): gracefully handle missing file_path in media downloads
items.content:
When Telegram API returns a file object without `file_path` (e.g., files still processing server-side), return `null` instead of throwing an error. This prevents agent crashes when users send media that hasn't finished processing yet.

**Product impact:**
- Users can now send recently uploaded/forwarded media without causing the agent to crash
- The message is processed without the media attachment, maintaining conversation flow
- Consistent with existing error handling for other recoverable media failures

**Changes:**
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media.ts`: Return `null` when `file_path` is missing instead of throwing
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media-retry.test.ts`: Updated test to verify graceful degradation

**Verification:**
- Updated existing test case to verify `null` return
- Aligns with existing pattern for `MediaFetchError` and size limit handling in bot-handlers.ts

items.author: github-actions[bot]

items.title: [Bug]: Telegram/webchat session routing cross-wire breaks operational continuity
items.content:
# OpenClaw bug report draft — Telegram/webchat session routing cross-wire

## Summary
Telegram direct messages are not reliably staying on their own Telegram session. Instead, they can collapse back into `agent:main:main` with `deliveryContext` effectively tied to webchat, causing replies and state continuity to become unreliable across Telegram and webchat.

This breaks the expected cross-surface workflow where a user should be able to continue the same project from webchat/dashboard and Telegram interchangeably.

## Impact
High.

For the affected setup:
- Telegram becomes unreliable as a control surface
- project state continuity across webchat and Telegram is broken
- automatic replies can route to the wrong surface or use stale context
- operational acceptance for real projects cannot be trusted even when repo/tests are green

## Environment
- OpenClaw: current local install on macOS (observed on 2026-03-22)
- Gateway: local LaunchAgent service
- Telegram channel: enabled, running, healthy according to `openclaw status`
- Webchat also active at the same time

## Expected behavior
- Telegram DM creates/uses a clean Telegram direct session
- webchat uses its own appropriate session path
- project state remains continuous across surfaces without stale context
- replies to Telegram-originated turns route back to Telegram
- a user can start in dashboard/webchat and continue in Telegram (and vice versa) without session corruption

## Actual behavior
Observed locally:
- Telegram direct session can appear briefly after restart
- then Telegram activity collapses back into `agent:main:main`
- `deliveryContext` / channel behavior drifts toward webchat
- Telegram and webchat state become inconsistent
- Telegram can answer from stale project state even after the repo/status files have moved on

Concrete observed state from `sessions_list` during failure:
- key: `agent:main:main`
- channel: `telegram`
- `deliveryContext.channel`: `webchat`
- `lastChannel`: `webchat`
- display

items.title: [Repo Assist] fix: apply NotificationSound setting to all toast notifications
items.content:
🤖 *This is an automated contribution from Repo Assist.*

## Root Cause

The `NotificationSound` setting in tray settings was never applied to toast notifications. All toast calls in `App.xaml.cs` called `new ToastContentBuilder()...Show()` directly, bypassing any sound preference.

## Fix

Introduces a `ShowToast(ToastContentBuilder)` helper in `App.xaml.cs` that checks `_settings.Settings.NotificationSound` before calling `.Show()`. When sound is disabled, the builder's `AddAudio` with `Silent = true` is applied before showing.

All 13 direct `.Show()` call sites in `App.xaml.cs` are routed through `ShowToast(...)` instead.

## Test Status

Tests could not be run in the Linux CI environment (project targets Windows). The logic is straightforward: one helper method added, all call sites updated to use it.

Closes #71

items.title: [Repo Assist] refactor: extract TryParseArgv helper in SystemCapability, fix operator precedence
items.content:
🤖 *This is an automated contribution from Repo Assist.*

Two related improvements to `SystemCapability` in `OpenClaw.Shared`.

## Changes

### 1. Extract `TryParseArgv` helper

`HandleRunPrepare` and `HandleRunAsync` both contained near-identical ~20-line JSON argv-parsing blocks. Both accept `command` as either a JSON string array or a single string. The logic is now consolidated into a single `private static bool TryParseArgv(JsonElement, out string[])` method, with the parsing contract documented in one place.

**Before:** ~40 lines of duplicated argv parsing across two methods.  
**After:** Two calls to `TryParseArgv`; each method focuses on its own concern.

### 2. Fix operator precedence in `HandleExecApprovalsSet`

The `enabled` boolean check was written as:
```csharp
if (ruleEl.TryGetProperty("enabled", out var enEl) && enEl.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.True || enEl.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.False)
```
`&&` binds tighter than `||`, so this evaluates as `(A && B) || C` rather than the intended `A && (B || C)`. Added parentheses to make the intent unambiguous.

## Test Status

Tests could not be run in the Linux CI environment (project targets Windows). The refactor is a pure structural extraction with no logic changes — the observable behaviour of `system.run`, `system.run.prepare`, and `system.execApprovals.set` is unchanged.

items.title: [tooling] Dedicated Repo Scanning Tool — Multi-backend Analysis
items.content:
> **Moved from** [neuron-box/db-issues#29](https://github.com/neuron-box/db-issues/issues/29)

## Problem Statement

Current approach: manual repomix usage to convert repos → markdown → NotebookLM sources.

**Gaps:**
1. Repomix is one tool; other extractors exist (Deep Wiki, context7, etc.)
2. No standardized output format or metadata
3. Manual orchestration each time
4. Missing: code structure analysis, dependency mapping, architecture overview

## Desired Output

### Core Deliverables
1. Code Structure — directory tree, module organization, entry points
2. Markdown Snapshot — full repo converted to readable markdown
3. Dependency Graph — package.json / requirements.txt analyzed
4. Architecture Overview — high-level system design from README, diagrams, key files
5. Key Functions/APIs — exported functions, classes, main entry points
6. Configuration — environment variables, config files, deployment notes
7. Tests & Quality — test coverage, CI/CD pipeline, quality gates

### Nice-to-Have
1. Commit History Snapshot — recent activity, contributors
2. Issue/PR Summary — known problems, active development areas
3. Examples/Quickstart — code examples from docs/tests
4. Performance Notes — benchmarks, scalability
5. Security Notes — known vulnerabilities, auth patterns

## Tool Candidates
- Repomix — markdown conversion
- Deep Wiki — structured repo exploration
- context7 — AI-optimized repo context extraction

## Proposed Solutions
Option A: Unified Wrapper CLI
Option B: OpenClaw Skill
Option C: Subagent Task

## Use Cases
1. OpenClaw ecosystem study for NotebookLM
2. Claw fleet bot mapping
3. Data-Israel analysis
4. Daily repo scanning automation

items.title: chore: promote dev→main — pgserve, fire-and-forget, scheduler, resume, stable fixes
items.content:
## Summary

Promotion of dev→main. 5 feature PRs + 1 bugfix PR included. 46 files changed, +6712/-181 lines.

### PRs Included
- **#673** — `pgserve-embed`: Embedded pgserve as genie's persistent brain
- **#674** — `fire-and-forget`: Fire-and-forget `genie work` + agent auto-exit
- **#675** — `genie-scheduler`: Scheduler daemon with lease-based trigger claiming
- **#677** — `genie-resume`: Session-resilient agent recovery with `genie resume`
- **#683** — `stable-release-fixes`: Cron step=0 guard, timezone support, lease-timeout in run_spec, sendMessage result check

### QA Evidence

- `bun run check`: **932 pass, 0 fail** (2019 expect() calls, 44 files)
- All grep assertions verified (removed patterns absent, added patterns present)
- All 4 bug issues (#678, #679, #680, #682) fixed and closed
- #681 closed as not-a-bug
- @next v3.260321.1 published and verified

### Key Features
- pgserve auto-starts on port 19642, schema migrations, `genie db` commands
- Fire-and-forget dispatch, mailbox delivery, agent auto-exit, push enforcement
- Scheduler daemon: LISTEN/NOTIFY, lease-based claiming, recurring triggers, systemd
- Agent resume: `genie resume`, auto-resume on crash, retry budget, cooldown
- Cron timezone support via `Intl.DateTimeFormat`

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run check` passes (932 tests, 0 failures)
- [x] All 4 QA council bugs fixed (#678, #679, #680, #682)
- [x] @next v3.260321.1 published and installable
- [x] Zero open bugs

items.title: [Bug]: web_search tool not available to agent despite correct configuration
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Brave Search plugin loads successfully and registers `web-search: brave` capability, but the `web_search` tool does not appear in the agent's available tools list.

### Steps to reproduce

Start openclaw with the following config for web_search tool.  API key properly configured (but redacted below):

## 

```json
{
  "tools": {
    "web": {
      "search": {
        "enabled": true,
        "provider": "brave"
      }
    }
  },
  "plugins": {
    "enabled": true,
    "allow": ["", "imessage", "bluebubbles", "brave"],
    "entries": {
      "brave": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "webSearch": {
            "apiKey": "<redacted>"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## 

### Expected behavior

`web_search` should appear in the agent's tool list and be callable.

### Actual behavior

Tool is not available. Agent reports:

> "The web_search tool isn't showing up in my available tools yet. I can see web_fetch (for extracting content from known URLs) and browser (for automation), but no search capability."

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.14 (which is newer than npm latest (2026.3.13) -- possible edge/dev build?

### Operating system

macOS 26.3

### Install method

docker via OrbStack

### Model

anthropic/claude-sonnet 4.5

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw via dashboard chat; on dedicated local machine via SSH tunnel 

### Additional provider/model setup details

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
# Plugin Status:
$ docker exec openclaw-gateway node openclaw.mjs plugins list | grep -i brave
│ Brave Plugin │ brave │ openclaw │ loaded │ stock:brave/index.ts │ 2026.3.14 │
│              │       │          │        │ Bundled Brave plugin │           │

# One potentially suspicious log entry (but unknown to me if this mode in fact prevents tool exposure):  

$ openclaw logs 2>&1 | grep -B 5 "Capabilities:"
Version: 2026.3.14
Shape: plain-capabi

items.title: Add ogham-mcp to Knowledge & Memory
items.content:
## Adding ogham-mcp

[ogham-mcp](https://github.com/ogham-mcp/ogham-mcp) -- persistent shared memory for AI agents.

- **Category:** Knowledge & Memory
- **Language:** Python
- **Scope:** Cloud + Local
- **Platforms:** macOS, Windows, Linux
- **License:** MIT

### What it does

Persistent, searchable shared memory across AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenClaw, and any MCP client). Memories persist across sessions and clients.

### Key features

- Hybrid search: pgvector cosine similarity + tsvector keyword matching via CCF fusion
- Knowledge graph with auto-linking and graph traversal
- Cognitive scoring (ACT-R temporal decay, confidence, graph boost)
- 16-language temporal extraction (no LLM needed)
- Severance-style memory profiles
- 97.2% Recall@10 on LongMemEval (ICLR 2025) with one PostgreSQL query -- no LLM in the retrieval loop
- Self-hosted (Supabase, Neon, vanilla Postgres) or managed cloud
- Docker, PyPI, uvx install options

### Links

- Repo: https://github.com/ogham-mcp/ogham-mcp
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/ogham-mcp/
- Website: https://ogham-mcp.dev
